title: vicia sativa f. rosiflora
intro: Vicia sativa f. rosiflora, also known as the garden pea or common vetch, is a subspecies of the annual legume Vicia sativa, characterized by its delicate, rose-pink flowers and edible seeds. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been attributed to the presence of various bioactive compounds, including flavonoids and phenolic acids. The medicinal properties of Vicia sativa f. rosiflora have been associated with several health benefits, including reducing oxidative stress, improving cardiovascular health, and exhibiting potential anticancer properties. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to support digestive health and alleviate symptoms of anxiety and insomnia.
